Our hosts ensured the environment remained light, brimming with warmth and encouragement. Their seamless coordination and lively interactions kept the audience on their toes, making the session as entertaining as it was informative.

In an inventive twist, the hosts infused a fun, interactive element into the quiz, ensuring maximum participation and energy from the audience. It was a testament to how our SAP family can shine even beyond their comfort zones, proving that talent, teamwork, and a touch of fun go hand-in-hand.

The Q&A session provided a platform for knowledge exchange, addressing key queries on SAP Business AI, data harmonization, and the future roadmap of BDC. The dialogue underscored the growing excitement and eagerness within the SAP community to explore the vast possibilities of our data-driven ecosystem.

Their dynamic hosting, filled with humour and encouragement, turned the session into a lively and interactive experience, proving that our SAP experts shine beyond their comfort zones. Every answer was spot on! – a true reflection of the audience’s deep understanding and excitement, which only grew with each passing question.

A lively and interactive BDC quiz session, keeping the audience engaged and excited.

Beyond the Celebration – A Look Ahead

As we wrapped up the event, one message was clear - SAP Business Data Cloud is not just an evolution: it’s a revolution.
With its powerful AI-driven analytics, seamless integration with SAP and non-SAP applications, and a commitment to unlocking business potential, BDC is set to be a cornerstone of AI-driven intelligent enterprise operations.
BDC is more than just a technological marvel - it is the driving force behind an enhanced customer experience. By simplifying complex processes, fostering real-time decision-making, and eliminating data silos, it empowers enterprises to be agile, responsive, and customer centric.
The event also helped the teams to connect during Lunch and Hi-Tea. This facilitated networking, fostering a sense of togetherness, and providing a platform for us to share the agile practises and key takeaways in turning this vision to a reality. It was a moment of shared success, reinforcing our commitment to continuous innovation.
The momentum didn’t stop there. On February 19, a week after the event, Sindhu Gangadharan - MD SAP Labs India | Head, Customer Innovation Services - connected with the entire BDCI team over a celebratory Hi-Tea. She congratulated the team on this critical milestone, emphasizing how their efforts played a pivotal role in SAP’s growth and long-term vision.

A celebratory hi-tea with the BDCI team, marking a milestone in SAP’s journey.

“The only way to do great work is to love what you do.” – Steve Jobs.
The passion and dedication that went into developing BDC reflect the essence of SAP’s vision - enabling businesses to thrive in the age of AI and intelligence.
This is not merely about data; it is about harnessing its power to transform businesses and elevate customer experiences, commitment, driving passion, technology, and engineering to unprecedented heights.

The BDCI team unites for a proud moment, celebrating a milestone in innovation and teamwork.
Cheers to every contributor, participant, and visionary who made the BDCI Celebration Event a grand success - a heartfelt thank you!

Let’s continue to innovate, disrupt, and redefine the future together!

Penned by,
Aishwarya BR
User Assistance Developer
BDC&I PTX UX Design Team, Bangalore

entry.title: Connect SAC with Azure Synapse SQL via SAC Agent using ActiveDirectoryPassword authentication
entry.content:
Introduction

This is the right guide in case you have the challenge to create an import connection to MS Synapse SQL DB which is only accessible from on-prem, due to security or other reasons.

For more general procedure I'd recommend to start with the SAP Blog from @kenneth_dalvik:

Tech Blog from Kenneth Dalvik 

The more detailed information you'll find in this blog entry. I'll describe in detail how to prepare SAC Agent for a successful connection using ActiveDirectoryPassword as authentication method (In the latest MS SQL driver it would refere to "With Microsoft Entra ID Password authentication using a login ID and password entered by the user.)

Remark: Configuration happened on a Windows server

Prerequisites:

  • Firewall setup completed that Synapse SQL DB accessible on Port 1433 from SAC Agent
  • Host from SAC Agent has access to login.microsoft.com domain
  • SAC Agent configured and DriverConfig.properties files maintained
  • A user that supports authentication method ActiveDirectoryPassword without MFA to Synapse SQL DB
  • JDBC driver compatible to Synapse SQL DB version
  • MSAL4J Java library and depended packages for ActiveDirectoryPassword auth method

Architecture:

This is the high level solution architecture, further details will be explained below

Configure SAC Agent:

Create the config file and place it in the Tomcat folder

<Tomcat path>\webapps\C4A_AGENT\configure\DriverConfig.properties

Example file is available here: SAC Help

Place the compatible jdbc driver (I've downloaded it fromsomewhere on the server convenient for you and copy the location of the file.

 

MS SQL Server 2019=<file path>\mssql-jdbc-12.8.1.jre11.jar

 

Create system variable on the server named SAP_CLOUD_AGENT_PROPERTIES_PATH

Add the java options parameter:

-DSAP_CLOUD_AGENT_PROPERTIES_PATH=<Tomcat path>\webapps\C4A_AGENT\configure\DriverConfig.properties

In order to allow usage of my desired authentication it was observed that additional java libraries were required. The library and all dependend libraries for msal4j have been added to <Tomcat path>\lib. The below listed items have been added:

  • accessors-smart-2.5.2.jar
  • apiguardian-api-1.1.2.jar
  • asm-9.7.1.jar
  • content-type-2.3.jar
  • jackson-annotations-2.18.1.jar
  • jackson-core-2.18.1.jar
  • jackson-databind-2.18.1.jar
  • jcip-annotations-1.0-1.jar
  • slf4j-api-1.7.36.jar
  • json-smart-2.5.2.jar
  • junit-jupiter-api-5.11.0.jar
  • junit-jupiter-params-5.11.0.jar
  • junit-platform-commons-1.11.0.jar
  • lang-tag-1.7.jar
  • msal4j-1.19.1.jar
  • nimbus-jose-jwt-10.0.1.jar
  • oauth2-oidc-sdk-11.23.jar
  • opentest4j-1.3.0.jar

->Restart the SAC Agent service.

 

Create SQL Database connection within SAC

In SAC frontent go to connections->connections  

Create a new connection and select SQL Database:

Enter connection parameters. Those connection parameters have been essential:

 

encrypt=true;trustServerCertificate=true;hostNameInCertificate=*.sql.azuresynapse.net;loginTimeout=30;authentication=ActiveDirectoryPassword;

 

Confirm with OK. Ideally the connection is saved and can be used later on.

I hope this prevents some headache and effort.

Please share your comments.

Thanks.

Best regards,

Timmy

entry.title: How to transport recurrent calendar Tasks between 2 different SAC Tenants
entry.content:
Some SAC customers asked me if it would be possible to transport recurrent calendar tasks (like data/multi actions tasks) cross tenants.

With the Q4.2024 SAC release (Plan Process Management - template support) it is possible but there are some steps that need to be followed in order to do that.

First you need to create a process on the Source Tenant and include the Data/Multi Action tasks within that (You will need to remove the recurrence to do that):

After creating the process, please create any general task with the left checkbox for the process selected:

You can select the Context Model for the task

Select the process again and now click on Save as Template

Save it on the folder that you are planning to transport (where the model and other objects are located)

Now it can be selected as an object for export within the transport menu

Proceed with the transport as usual and you will see the template saved on the target tenant:

Open the Process Template and click on Create Process from Template:

Now you should have a process with the calendar tasks on your calendar:

If you need to put recurrence on the calendar task you should remove the parent dependency from it:

OR

Now the Add Recurrence button should be enabled:

Configure the recurrence as required and update the task

The generated process can be deleted in case you don’t need it anymore:

Go to the Series Menu and rename it accordingly

Now you should have your Calendar Tasks transported properly.
For more information on this topic please check Work with Calendar Templates - SAC Help page
